description: Quest chains shown on the /onboarding page. Data-driven so new quests can be added without touching the page code. Progress is tracked per step in the user's localStorage under the key 'humanity_quest_progress'. Updated for v0.105+ post-quantum architecture: DIDs, Verifiable Credentials, multi-layer trust score, social key recovery, governance, and AI-as-citizen.

chains.title: Welcome Quest
chains.description: Your first ten minutes on HumanityOS.

steps.title: Open the Chat
steps.description: Pick a display name, say hi in #welcome. A post-quantum cryptographic identity (Dilithium3) is created on your device automatically.

steps.title: Fill in your Profile
steps.description: Add a display name, bio, and avatar. This is how others see you across the network. Your DID stays the same regardless.

steps.title: Back up your 24-word seed phrase
steps.description: Write the seed down in Settings. It deterministically derives both your post-quantum identity AND your optional Solana wallet. Lose your phone, recover everything.

steps.title: Try the Real / Sim toggle
steps.description: The pill in the top-right swaps between your real-life data and the simulation. Click it once to see the difference.

chains.title: Identity & Trust
chains.description: Build out your identity. Earn vouches. See how the multi-layer trust score works. Anti-Sybil math is transparent — every input visible.

steps.title: Find your DID
steps.description: In Settings, find your did:hum:<base58> identifier. Short enough to share via QR. Resolves to your current Dilithium3 public key on any federated server.

steps.title: Vouch for a friend
steps.description: Open someone's profile and click 'Vouch'. This issues a vouch_v1 Verifiable Credential — they hold it, choose when to share it. Vouching from many distinct communities raises their trust score with anti-farm math.

steps.title: Browse your credentials
steps.description: See every Verifiable Credential about you: vouches, server membership, skills, account age. You can withdraw any one to hide it from display without invalidating the record.

steps.title: Check your trust score
steps.description: A 0..1 score combining vouches, credentials, activity diversity, and account age. Inputs are always visible — no black-box reputation. Hover over each sub-score to see exactly what fed it.

steps.title: Set up social key recovery
steps.description: Pick 3 to 5 trusted contacts as guardians. Your seed is split via Shamir secret sharing, encrypted to each guardian's Kyber768 key, stored opaquely on the relay. Lose your device, recover by getting M-of-N guardians to send their shares.

chains.title: Explore the Platform
chains.description: One small task for each core page. Skim-friendly, takes about fifteen minutes.

steps.title: Create a task
steps.description: Open the kanban board and add one card. Real or Sim. Something you actually want to track.

steps.title: Browse the Marketplace
steps.description: See what people are offering. Trust score gates high-value listings (anti-fraud) but anyone can browse. Check a listing, read a review, message a seller.

steps.title: Open your Wallet (optional)
steps.description: If you want to use Solana for payments or tips, your wallet is derived from the same 24-word seed via a separate path. Skip if you never want to use blockchain — HumanityOS works without it.

steps.title: Check the Maps page
steps.description: See what is around you. Real mode shows the real world. Sim mode shows the game world.

steps.title: Write a note
steps.description: Notes are encrypted on your device with AES-256-GCM and an Argon2id-derived key. Useful for journals, drafts, or anything you want private.

chains.title: Civic Participation
chains.description: How decisions get made. Local server rules, civilization-wide Accord changes, weighted voting capped to prevent any single voice from dominating.

steps.title: Read open proposals
steps.description: Every server has its own proposal space. Civilization-scope proposals (Accord amendments, federation floor policy) federate to all servers. Browse what's open right now.

steps.title: Cast a vote
steps.description: Pick yes / no / abstain. Your vote weight is your trust score at vote time, capped at 0.95. The cap prevents any single high-trust voter from dominating — Accord power-asymmetry protection.

steps.title: Watch a tally update
steps.description: Tallies are deterministic given the vote set. You can replay any past proposal's outcome from the signed-object record and verify the result yourself.

steps.title: Note: AI agents cannot vote
steps.description: Per the Accord, votes require sentient consent. AI agents (declared via subject_class_v1) are silently excluded from governance — they can advise their human operator, but cannot themselves vote.

chains.title: Choose Who You Interact With
chains.description: AI agents are first-class citizens of HumanityOS, but humans always retain the right to refuse AI interaction. Configure your preferences here.

steps.title: Spot the AI badge
steps.description: AI agents always render with a persistent 'AI' tag next to their display name. They must declare their subject_class_v1 and link to a human operator via controlled_by_v1.

steps.title: Set your interaction filter
steps.description: In Profile settings, choose: any (default — see everyone), human_only (filter AI from your view, with count of hidden items shown), or ai_disclosed_ok (allow AI when introduced, hide silent ones).

steps.title: AI must announce itself
steps.description: When an AI starts a new conversation with you, its first message must be an ai_introduction_v1 declaring itself. Failing to do so is grounds for trust penalty.

chains.title: Going Deeper
chains.description: When you are ready to get more out of the platform, or help build it.

steps.title: Install the desktop app
steps.description: Native Rust binary that works offline. Same DID as the web version. Syncs when you reconnect. wgpu 3D engine bundled.

steps.title: Join a federated server
steps.description: Other communities run their own servers. Your DID works on all of them. VCs you hold travel with you. Find one whose culture fits.

steps.title: Issue a credential
steps.description: Anyone can be an issuer. Schools, employers, communities, individuals. Issue a vouch_v1, role_v1, skill_endorsement_v1, or any other supported schema. The receiver decides if and when to share it.

steps.title: Run your own server
steps.description: One small binary. Under ten minutes from zero to live. Your users keep their DID when they switch servers. You become an issuer too.

steps.title: Contribute to the project
steps.description: Code, writing, design, translation, testing, feedback. The onboarding guide shows where to start.

steps.title: Read the Humanity Accord
steps.description: The philosophical foundation of the project. Defines the constraints every system must respect: dignity, non-domination, consent, transparency, repair-first, power-asymmetry mitigation. Short, plain-language, not a legal document.
